design_philosophy.core_loop: Create custom boxer → fight through ranked opponents in career mode → train between fights to improve attributes → progress toward title match against Evander Holyfield → defend title in challenge matches
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing fighter development (training/attributes) against career longevity (fighters weaken and retire after ~40 fights), preventing infinite grinding on weak opponents
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Build and manage a custom boxer's career from undercard fighter to heavyweight champion, with visible progression through rankings and realistic stat degradation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces career mortality and attribute decay—players cannot indefinitely farm weak opponents for training advantage. After ~20-25 fights, boxer begins weakening; mandatory retirement at 40 fights. Defeated Holyfield himself declines in rankings. This creates strategic tension around when to challenge for title vs. build experience. Scoring system is deliberately unrealistic (10-9 rounds unless knockdown occurs), prioritizing knockout/damage-based victory conditions over boxing judging authenticity.

difficulty.lives_system: Knockout system: three knockdowns end fight; alternatively TKO triggered by extreme head/body damage or boxer refusing to engage in combat
difficulty.checkpointing: Battery-backed RAM saves career progress between fights; players can pause mid-fight to taunt opponents
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Significant control via training system—players select training activities (free weights, protein diet, karate lessons, health club visits, iron gum shield) to increase power, speed, stamina, and defense attributes. Custom boxer creation allows selection of body size and handedness. Difficulty scales implicitly as opponent ranks increase in career mode, but no explicit difficulty settings documented.

progression.upgrade_system: Attribute training between fights: power, speed, stamina, defense increased through selectable training regimens (free weights, nutrition, karate, health club, equipment). Each boxer has baseline attributes; Evander Holyfield has maximum ratings in all four attributes
progression.unlock_mechanism: Secret boxer 'The Beast' unlocked by entering that name during boxer creation. Rankings gradually replace aging/low-ranked boxers with new fighters as career advances
replayability_hooks: 28 fictional boxers with varying attributes and difficulty progression
Exhibition mode allows any-vs-any matchups with human or AI control
Multiple custom boxer builds possible with different body types, colors, handedness combinations
Career mode reset required to restore Holyfield to prime form after defeat
Strategic replayability around training choices and title challenge timing before attribute decay begins

world_building_techniques: Fictional boxer roster with names and nationalities establishing boxing world context
Career progression through ranking system (28 fighters ranked from challenger to champion)
Taunt dialogue during fights ('come on and fight you wimp!') provides minimal personality
Title defense system creates narrative arc (overcome ranked opponents → challenge Holyfield → defend title against high-ranked contenders)

boss_design.philosophy: Single climactic opponent (Evander Holyfield) serves as final ranked challenge; player must defeat all 28 boxers to reach title match. Rather than mechanically unique boss, Holyfield represents difficulty peak through maximum attribute ratings (power, speed, stamina, defense all maxed). Post-title, challenge matches against unnamed 'high-ranked contenders' provide ongoing difficulty scaling
notable_bosses: Evander Holyfield (World Heavyweight Champion, final ranking opponent, maximum stats in all attributes, serves as title holder and final career goal)

faction_indicators: Custom boxer creation with cosmetic customization (skin color, shorts color, body size) allows self-expression
Competitive multiplayer mode supports player-vs-player matches
Ranking system creates status hierarchy among 28 boxers
danger_signals: Cosmetic customization colors lack demographic specificity but include unconventional options (purple, gold, cyan boxing attire) suggesting cartoonish tone over realism
Opponent roster entirely composed of fictional boxers with no ethnic or cultural stereotyping documented
environmental_storytelling: Ring design and overhead map provide spatial context for 360-degree movement mechanics
Training facility options (free weights, health club, karate dojo) implied through menu selections rather than visual environments
Crowd audio effects (reaction to damaging punches) create ambient fight atmosphere
